shelf_mappings.mapping_rationale: Semi-hard and hard yellow block cheeses (Emek, Gouda, Emmental, Gruyere). Primary Israeli sandwich cheese shelf. Excluded from cheese_spreads (confirmed: bsip1/run_cheese_003 excluded barcode 8866972 גבינה גרוויר as yellow_hard_processed_excluded).
Pre-packaged sliced yellow cheeses (Emek slices, Gouda slices). Same formulation as block, different form factor. No overlap with cheese_spreads.

Reduced-fat variants of yellow cheese, typically 9-16% fat per 100g vs 28-31% in full-fat. Often contain added stabilizers/starches. Distinct from full-fat pool by fat level and ingredient complexity.
Bulgarian-style white brined cheese (גבינה בולגרית), blocks or vacuum packs. Semi-hard, high-sodium, sold as separate product type. No overlap with cheese_spreads (labaneh/cottage are excluded).
Tzfatit-style cheese (גבינה צפתית), Israeli artisanal tradition, similar to Bulgarian but distinct brand identity. Semi-hard, brined.
Hard aged cheeses for grating (Parmesan-style), pre-grated or block. High protein (28-33g/100g), high sodium (1370-1600mg/100g). Distinct shelf section in Israeli supermarkets.

Processed cheese slices and blocks (American singles, sandwich cheese). Contains added emulsifiers, phosphates, vegetable oils. Classified as sub-pool within hard_cheeses per TASK-215 scope, not separate category.

conflict_check.cheese_spreads: CLEAR — cheese_spreads covers cottage, labaneh, quark, cream cheese. Hard yellow, Bulgarian, Tzfatit, Parmesan, processed slices are out-of-scope for cheese_spreads. Confirmed via run_cheese_003 exclusion list.
